full_text:
Heterogeneity and treatment landscape of ovarian carcinoma

Notes: Solid introduction to the heterogeneity observed in HGSC, and discussion of treatments

The drug efficacy testing in 3D cultures platform identifies effective drugs for ovarian cancer patients

Note: Everyone agreed that this paper was well written and easy to follow. We discussed designing models specific to the questions we aim to investigate. The functional precision medicine platform described in this paper is a short-term patient derived culture very similar to the iPDCs established in our lab. However, the fPM model focused on developing organoids composed of tumor and stromal cells while disregarding immune cells. We discussed that this a significant shortcoming given the potential synergistic effect of DNA damaging drugs (DDD) and immunotherapies. In addition, the exclusion immune cells may underestimate the cytotoxic off-target effect of DDD combinations. Moving forward in the iPDC project, we aim to add fibroblasts to our cultures to study the interplay of tumor, immune, and stromal cells in an ex vivo model. In addition, we strive to test more combination therapies at different concentrations to identify effective tumor-specific novel therapies and combination therapies.
